title: Full-Cycle Subscription Exploitation with Terminal Month Chargeback
narrative: Customer enrolls in monthly subscription service (streaming, software, meal kit) at $29.99-$79.99/month, uses service throughout billing cycle including final week, then disputes charge 25-29 days post-transaction claiming "unauthorized recurring charge." Pattern shows normal usage behavior: streaming 15-40 hours/week, software logins 4-6 days/week, or meal deliveries accepted. Chargeback filed 1-3 days before next billing cycle would initiate. Cardholder typically disputes only most recent charge while keeping subscription active or re-enrolling under different email. Common in ProductCD='W' (digital services) with 60-90 day subscription histories. Exploits issuer bias toward cardholders on recurring billing disputes despite clear service consumption.
indicators: Chargeback filed 25-29 days after TransactionAmt posted, timing correlates with end of service period
card1_txn_count_28day shows 2-12 prior identical recurring charges same MID, all successful
Dispute reason code 4853/4541 ("not as described" or "recurring transaction cancelled") despite no cancellation request in merchant logs
Service utilization logs show 12+ access events during disputed billing period, including activity 1-4 days before chargeback
P_emaildomain differs from email on 1-3 prior subscription periods same card1, suggesting serial re-enrollment
TransactionAmt matches merchant's standard monthly tier pricing exactly ($29.99, $49.99, $79.99), not prorated or discounted
No failed authorization attempts or card1_amt_zscore anomalies preceding dispute, indicating normal payment acceptance
summary: A customer uses a subscription service normally throughout the entire billing month, then files a chargeback 25-29 days after payment claiming the charge was unauthorized or the subscription was cancelled, despite logs showing active usage up until days before the dispute.
reasoning: The customer is deliberately timing the chargeback to extract a full month of service for free while appearing as a victim of unauthorized billing, exploiting card issuers' tendency to side with cardholders on recurring charge disputes even when service consumption is documented.
indicator_explanations: Chargeback filed precisely when subscription service period ends, suggesting planned timing
Card successfully paid identical monthly charges before suddenly disputing the latest one
Customer claims service issue but never contacted merchant to cancel or complain
User actively consumed the service throughout the month they're now disputing
Email address changed between subscription cycles indicating potential serial abuse pattern
Amount is standard full-month pricing, customer received complete billing period of service
Payment processed normally with no payment issues, then disputed after service consumed
